items.title: Boeing Is Getting More Orders. The Stock Is Going Down.
items.description: Boeing  is having a solid showing at the Dubai Air Show.  The move comes despite new orders for the long-delayed 777x jet and Boeing’s 787 Dreamliner.  Tuesday, Boeing announced that Gulf Air finalized an order for 15 787 Dreamliner jets.

items.title: Trump to Sell F-35s to Saudi Arabia. Why Lockheed Stock Is Rising.
items.description: Jefferies analyst Sheila Kahyaoglu estimates about $4 billion in sales and $1.50 in EPS for the aerospace company from sales of the jets to the kingdom.

items.title: flydubai Dumps Boeing for 150 Airbus Jets in Stunning Pivot
items.description: Airbus lands its biggest Middle East single-aisle win after years of Boeing dominance falter.

items.title: Dubai Airshow: Flying Taxi Startup Debuts, Airbus Nabs Boeing Clients
items.description: The aerospace industry turns its attention to the second day of the Dubai Airshow. The world's largest aerospace companies — jet builders, engine manufactures and military contractors that design fighter planes — are all gathering at the weeklong Dubai Airshow, which runs through Friday. Companies will showcase new products and compete for some of the industry's most lucrative contracts. Tuesday...

items.title: Is LMT Expanding Helicopter Capabilities With Autonomous Upgrades?
items.description: Lockheed Martin expands Black Hawk capabilities with MATRIX autonomy and the new U-Hawk to advance multi-mission rotorcraft operations.
